data.title: Package DSO ticket system for external distribution
data.description:
## Context

The DSO ticket system — event-sourced log on an orphan `tickets` branch, reducer-computed state, Jira bridge, optimistic-concurrency STATUS transitions — is a general-purpose LLM-first ticket tracker conceptually decoupled from the DSO plugin itself. Multiple external teams have informally expressed interest in adopting it standalone. Packaging it as a distributable library (pip-installable or npm-equivalent shell script bundle) would let non-DSO adopters consume it without installing the full DSO plugin. This epic does the extraction and packaging work.

## Success Criteria
- Boundary audit confirms zero imports from `plugins/dso/scripts/` outside the ticket system into the ticket system itself (enforced by `check-plugin-self-ref.sh` equivalent).
- Ticket system ships as an independently installable package with its own semver-released artifacts (tarball or wheel). Installation into a bare git repo produces a working `ticket init`, `ticket create`, `ticket show`, `ticket list`, `ticket transition`, `ticket link`, `ticket deps`.
- Jira bridge ships as an optional plugin (extra-dependency) rather than a required dependency; non-Jira adopters install the core only.
- Reference CLI documentation, migration guide, and contribution guide shipped with the package.
- Integration test: in a scratch git repository, install the package, create an epic with two child stories, transition through states, run a Jira sync (mocked), and verify event log structure via reducer.
- External adopter docs explain: required git layout, orphan `tickets` branch creation, environment variables, and migration from legacy ticket systems.

## Approach
Identify all cross-module dependencies (`_PLUGIN_ROOT` references, sourcing of DSO-only libraries, config file paths) and extract the ticket system into a self-contained subtree. Introduce a stable public API surface for the reducer and bridge abstractions. Use `pyproject.toml` for the Python portions (reducer, bridge) and a shell script bundle for the CLI dispatcher. Jira bridge becomes `dso-ticket-bridge-jira` as an extras package. Include a migration script for existing DSO-plugin users to switch from in-plugin scripts to the standalone package without event-log disruption.

## Dependencies
- Blocks on e1b2-2653 (ticket system boundary enforcement and documentation sweep) — until the boundary is enforced, extraction is infeasible.
- Depends on 78fc-3858 (sourceable ticket-lib API) — external consumers should use the library API rather than subprocess CLI calls.

## Out of Scope
- Multi-repo aggregation/federation
- Web UI (the CLI + Jira are the only supported interfaces)
- Non-Jira bridges (GitHub Issues, Linear, etc.) — possible future extras packages
